violations.text: French Fries, cooked previous day, were not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Several covered plastic tubs of French Fries were observed held at 45 to 52 degrees Fahrenheit in the walk-in cooler. French Fries were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025

Several food items in the sliding door cooler and the middle prep unit were observed held at 44 to 50 degrees Fahrenheit (See Temperatures). Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Based on temperature observed at and time food items were prepped, food items were moved to colder refrigeration at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025

Previously prepared Garlic Butter, which was prepared on site, was observed held beyond 7 days from the date of preparation. Time/Temperature Control for Safety Foods prepared on site are to be marked with the date of preparation and held for no longer than 7 days from the date of preparation unless frozen. Garlic Butter was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025

A spray bottle of Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Spray bottle was removed from use at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025

The door to the outside, located in the back dry storage area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2025

The ice scoop was observed stored on an unclean surface (prep counter) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Ice scoop was moved to dish area for cleaning at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025

At the time of inspection, the following equipment was observed in need of repair... - The sliding door cooler was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 41 to 51 degrees Fahrenheit from top to bottom. Unit is not to be used for storage of Time/Temperature Control for Safety Foods until adjusted/serviced/repaired/replaced. - The gaskets to the sliding door cooler and the Fry Area Prep Cooler were observed to be cracked/torn and in need of replacement. Equipment is to be maintained in good repair New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris... - Refrigeration door gaskets - Sliding door cooler door tracks - Shelving - Hood vents/grates (greasy) - cookline equipment Equipment is to be cleaned as often as necessary to be cleaned and to preclude the buildup of debris. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2025

The following areas of the physical facilities were observed in need of repair/adjustment. - The ceiling in the area directly outside the walk-in cooler is peeling and in need of repair - The ceiling in the kitchen area is missing a tile over the cookline area - Wall tiles at corners/wall junctures were observed to be cracked/missing The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2025

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in the kitchen, prep, and storage areas. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2025
